John, a junior software engineer. He studied computer science for his undergraduate degree at the University of Bristol and recently graduated. He did an internship in a small startup at the end of his third year where he assisted senior developers to build a tool to automatically generate invoices for people in the creative industries. He has experience with Python, AWS, and unit testing.

Elizabeth, a principal software engineer at Amazon. She has 12 years of professional software development experience and has led large-scale projects in industry. She has knowledge of object-oriented design, data structures, and algorithms. She also has experience designing and building large-scale systems in a multi-tiered, distributed environment. In her current role at the Amazon Device Management Systems organisation, she works on device-authentication, device-registration, device profiles and account-linking and is now working to expand to Remote Key provisioning and Passkeys. Her PhD research at ETH Zurich focused on technical implementation of FIDO Alliance-based Passkeys.

Brad, a Senior DevOps engineer with five years of experience at Footprint IT Solutions in London, a tech consultancy. In this role, he provisions cloud services using Infrastructure as Code and configures them for optimum performance. He is proficient at creating and operating continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines to deliver improvements. He has an AWS certification in Solutions Architecture. Prior to his current dev ops role, he was a consultant at McKinsey, advising companies on digital transformation. He has a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from the University of Southern California.

Emma, a fellow in Astrophysics at Selwyn College, Cambridge. She holds a bachelor’s and master’s degree in Physics from Imperial College London and a PhD in Astrophysics from the University of Cambridge. Emma is a radio astronomer whose research investigates the origin and evolution of large-scale cosmic magnetic fields. She has many years of experience through her studies and research using Python, C, Fortran and Matlab for data analysis and simulation. She has built and deployed with her team several python packages for radio astronomy, such as for simulating radio interferometers and for representing diffuse, extended and compact astrophysical radio sources.

Michael, a Research Economist at Citibank. In this role, he provides analyses of economic, policy, and political developments to assist Citi’s clients and businesses in making investment decisions. He focuses specifically on analysis of the German economy, providing detailed economic analysis and forecasts. He builds his models using MatLab, Stat, EViews, and Excel. He has an MPhil in Economics from the London School of Economics where he specialised in monetary policy in developing economies.

Sarah, a historian with a PhD from the University of Warwick. Her research focus was the history of political thought. She currently works as a Heritage Project Manager for Hull City Council with responsibility for refurbishment of maritime sites and historic ships. She manages the budget and project delivery and reports to external stakeholders. Outside of this Sarah consults with not-for-profit organisations and local governments, assisting them with history projects. She has published several research papers on imperialism and comparative politics.

Harvey, a social media manager for L'oreal Paris. He is responsible for developing social media strategies that increase brand awareness and customer engagement, building an engaged community of users, launching innovative and buzzworthy campaigns, and analysing performance and engagement metrics. Harvey creates content across multiple platforms including Instagram, TikTok, BlueSky and LinkedIn. He has a BAchelor of Arts from the Royal College of Art and a Foundation degree in Design and Applied Arts from London Metropolitan University. Harvey launched a popular instagram account called Dogs Wearing Crocs in New York which was later published as a book.

Hannah, a tax accountant at PWC. She studied Business Administration at Stockholm University and later qualified as a Chartered Accountant. Hannah has worked for PWC for 15 years in this capacity and advises clients on tax related matters. She specialises in high net worth clients with complicated family trusts.

John, an intellectual property lawyer for Canadian fashion brand, Aritzia. He studied history at the University of Victoria and then law at the University of Toronto. John also runs an Etsy storefront selling upcycled tin cans that he has repurposed into candles with motivational quotes on them.
